title: Passing Parameters
description: Send data between screens via route params.

slides.title: Why Params? 📦
slides.content:
# Sending Data to a Screen

Often you need to pass data from one screen to another – e.g., an item ID to a details screen.

In React Navigation, you pass parameters as a second argument to `navigate`:

```jsx
navigation.navigate('Details', { itemId: 86, otherParam: 'something' });
```

slides.title: Receiving Params

quizData.question: How do you access parameters passed to a screen?
quizData.explanation: The screen receives a `route` prop, and params are inside `route.params`.

slides.title: Updating Params
slides.content:
## Changing Params from the Screen

You can also update the params of the current screen using `navigation.setParams`:

```jsx
navigation.setParams({ otherParam: 'updated!' });
```

This is useful for things like toggling favourite status.

slides.title: Important ⚠️
slides.content: Params should be simple JSON‑serializable data (strings, numbers, booleans, arrays, objects). Avoid passing functions or non‑serializable values, as they can break navigation state persistence.
